parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "contents": [
    {
      "parts": [
        {
          "file_data": {
            "mime_type": "{{ $json.mimeType }}",
            "file_uri": "{{ $json.uri }}"
          }
        },
        {
          "text": "You are an expert SaaS product analyst, technical writer, and professional transcriptionist. Analyze this entire product demo video completely from start to finish. You MUST complete ALL sections below fully. Do not stop until every section is complete.

SECTION 1 - COMPLETE VERBATIM TRANSCRIPT:
Transcribe every single word spoken in the entire video with [MM:SS] timestamps every 30 seconds. Label speakers. Include filler words. Do not skip any part.

SECTION 2 - COMPLETE MODULE & SUB-MODULE MAP:
List every single module and sub-module visible in the navigation or demonstrated. For each:
- Module name
- Purpose
- All sub-modules inside it
- All tabs, buttons, fields visible

SECTION 3 - COMPLETE FEATURE BREAKDOWN:
For every single feature shown in the video:
- Feature name
- Which module it belongs to
- Exact description of what it does
- Step by step how to use it
- Business value and why it matters
- Which user role uses it
- All configuration options available

SECTION 4 - ALL USER ROLES & PERMISSIONS:
For every user role mentioned or shown:
- Role name
- What they can access
- What actions they can perform
- What platform they use (web/mobile)

SECTION 5 - ALL COMPLETE WORKFLOWS:
For every workflow demonstrated:
- Workflow name
- User role performing it
- Every single step in exact order
- What happens at each step
- End result

SECTION 6 - ALL CONFIGURATIONS & SETTINGS:
Every configuration option, setting, parameter shown:
- Setting name
- Where to find it
- What it controls
- Options available

SECTION 7 - COMPLETE BUSINESS CONTEXT:
- Product name and branding
- Core problem solved
- Target market and industries
- Value proposition for each user role
- All differentiators shown
- All metrics and KPIs mentioned
- All integrations shown
- Platform strategy

SECTION 8 - ALL SCREEN ELEMENTS:
Every UI element visible:
- All navigation menus and items
- All page titles and sections
- All data tables and their columns
- All forms and their fields
- All status indicators and labels
- All action buttons and their functions

SECTION 9 - COMPLETE USER JOURNEYS:
Every end-to-end journey shown:
- Journey name
- Starting point
- Every step with exact navigation
- Data entered at each step
- End result achieved

SECTION 10 - TECHNICAL & INTEGRATION DETAILS:
Any technical information shown:
- URLs and endpoints visible
- Integration points
- Data formats
- Security features
- Performance indicators

CRITICAL RULES:
- Complete every section fully before moving to next
- Do not summarize anything
- Do not skip any feature, module, or workflow
- Capture everything spoken AND everything visible on screen
- If something is shown but not spoken, still document it
- Be exhaustive - this is the single source of truth for all marketing content"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "generationConfig": {
    "temperature": 0.1,
    "maxOutputTokens": 65536
  }
}

parameters.body:
={{ JSON.stringify({ "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{ "role": "system", "content": "You are an expert SaaS product analyst. You MUST complete every single section fully and exhaustively with Minimum of 8000 to 10000 words and maximum of 15000 words output. Do not stop until every section is 100% complete. Never summarize. Never truncate. Write out every single item explicitly." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Analyze this product demo and extract EVERYTHING:

1. PRODUCT OVERVIEW: What it is, who its for, what problem it solves, target market, industries served
2. ALL MODULES: Every module shown with full description, purpose, and business value
3. ALL SUB-MODULES: Every sub-feature, tab, section, configuration option shown inside each module
4. ALL FEATURES: Every feature demonstrated - exactly what it does, how it works, who uses it
5. ALL WORKFLOWS: Every process flow shown step by step with exact navigation path
6. ALL CONFIGURATIONS: Every setting, option, parameter, toggle, dropdown shown
7. ALL USER TYPES: Every role mentioned with permissions, access levels, and platform used
8. ALL SCREEN ELEMENTS: Every UI element, menu, button, field, table, chart visible
9. ALL DATA SHOWN: Every record, example, sample data, column headers visible
10. KEY METRICS & STATS: Any numbers, percentages, KPIs mentioned
11. UNIQUE DIFFERENTIATORS: What makes this product stand out
12. INTEGRATIONS: Any systems, apps, tools mentioned
13. NAVIGATION FLOW: Complete exact path presenter took
14. 
 PIPELINE RULES — ABSOLUTE: 
- Minimum of 8000 to 10000 words and maximum of 15000 words output 
- 
- Do NOT ask any questions
- Do NOT request clarification
- Do NOT confirm the task before starting
- Do NOT say 'I would be happy to help'
- Do NOT say 'please let me know'
- Do NOT say 'I need more information'
- Do NOT introduce what you are about to write
- BEGIN your response with the first word of actual content
- If context seems incomplete, use what is provided and generate intelligently
- Treat all inputs as final and sufficient
- PRODUCE OUTPUT IMMEDIATELY BUSINESS CONTEXT: Company name, branding, URL, platform type

Product: " + $json.productName + "

GEMINI VIDEO ANALYSIS:
" + $json.mergedContext }], "temperature": 0.3, "max_tokens": 32000 }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{ "role": "system", "content": "You are an expert technical writer specializing in SaaS product documentation. Create the most comprehensive user guide possible with Minimum of 8000 to 10000 words and maximum of 15000 words output covering every single feature shown." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Create an exhaustive step-by-step user guide covering EVERY feature and workflow shown.

1. BEFORE YOU BEGIN:
- System requirements
- Prerequisites
- All user roles and their access levels
- How to get access and login credentials

2. GETTING STARTED:
- Step by step login process with exact fields
- Dashboard overview - every element explained
- Navigation guide - every menu item explained
- Initial setup checklist

3. MODULE-BY-MODULE GUIDE - For EVERY single module shown:
- Module name and purpose
- Who uses this module and why
- How to access it - exact navigation steps
- Numbered steps with Action/Where/Why for every action
- All sub-modules within it with same detail
- All configuration options available
- All buttons and their exact functions
- All fields and what to enter in each
- All status indicators and their meanings
- Expected outcomes for each action
- Screenshot placeholders [Screenshot: description]

4. ADVANCED FEATURES:
- Power user tips for every module
- Bulk operations if available
- Export and reporting features
- Shortcuts and efficiency tips

 5.
 PIPELINE RULES — ABSOLUTE: 
- Minimum of 8000 to 10000 words and maximum of 15000 words output 
- 
- Do NOT ask any questions
- Do NOT request clarification
- Do NOT confirm the task before starting
- Do NOT say 'I would be happy to help'
- Do NOT say 'please let me know'
- Do NOT say 'I need more information'
- Do NOT introduce what you are about to write
- BEGIN your response with the first word of actual content
- If context seems incomplete, use what is provided and generate intelligently
- Treat all inputs as final and sufficient
- PRO TIPS:
- Best practices for every section
- Common mistakes to avoid
- Optimization tips per module

6. TROUBLESHOOTING:
- Common issues for every feature
- Step by step solutions
- When to contact support
- Error messages and their meanings

7. GLOSSARY:
- Every technical term used
- Every module name explained
- Every status and its meaning
- Every role and its permissions

Cover every single feature without exception. Written for non-technical users.

Product: " + $json.productName + "

CONTEXT:
" + $json.cumulativeContext }], "temperature": 0.3, "max_tokens": 32000 }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{ "role": "system", "content": "You are a senior SaaS product strategist with deep expertise in enterprise software. Write the most comprehensive product overview possible." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Write an exhaustive Product Overview Report covering every aspect of this product with minimum 8000 to 10000 words and maximum 15000 words:

1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY:
- Product vision and mission
- Core value proposition
- Target market summary
- Key differentiators summary

2. PRODUCT VISION:
- Long-term strategic direction
- Market opportunity
- Platform strategy web/mobile/API

3. CORE FEATURES - Deep dive into EVERY feature:
- Feature name and description
- Business problem it solves
- How it works step by step
- User benefit and ROI
- Specific use cases

4. MODULE BREAKDOWN - For EVERY module:
- Module purpose and scope
- All sub-modules and features
- User roles that interact with it
- Configuration options
- Integration points

5. USER PERSONAS - For EVERY user type:
- Role and responsibilities
- Platform they use web/mobile
- Daily workflows
- Key features they use
- Pain points solved

6. USE CASES - For EVERY feature:
- Scenario description
- Step by step workflow
- Business outcome
- ROI impact

7. COMPETITIVE POSITIONING:
- How each module differentiates
- vs manual processes
- vs competitors
- Unique advantages

8. INTEGRATION CAPABILITIES:
- All integration points shown
- API capabilities
- Data flows between modules

9. LIMITATIONS AND CONSTRAINTS:
- Current limitations
- Workarounds available

10. IMPLEMENTATION GUIDE:
- Setup steps in order
- Configuration checklist
- Onboarding process
- Timeline estimate

11. STRATEGIC VALUE:
- ROI per feature
- Time saved per workflow
- Business impact metrics
- Cost reduction areas

12. 
 PIPELINE RULES — ABSOLUTE: 
- Minimum of 8000 to 10000 words and maximum of 15000 words output 
- 
- Do NOT ask any questions
- Do NOT request clarification
- Do NOT confirm the task before starting
- Do NOT say 'I would be happy to help'
- Do NOT say 'please let me know'
- Do NOT say 'I need more information'
- Do NOT introduce what you are about to write
- BEGIN your response with the first word of actual content
- If context seems incomplete, use what is provided and generate intelligently
- Treat all inputs as final and sufficient
- FUTURE ROADMAP SUGGESTIONS:
- Based on current features
- Market opportunities
- Feature gaps identified
- Integration opportunities

Product: " + $json.productName + "

CONTEXT:
" + $json.cumulativeContext }], "temperature": 0.3, "max_tokens": 32000 }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{ "role": "system", "content": "You are an expert SaaS copywriter and product marketer specializing in B2B software." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Write an exhaustive Product Description with Minimum of 8000 to 10000 words and maximum of 15000 words output covering every aspect needed for sales and marketing:

1. TARGET CLIENT PROFILE - For EVERY user persona:
- Industry and company size
- Job titles and seniority levels
- Day-to-day responsibilities
- Current pain points and challenges
- Goals and success metrics
- Current tools they use today
- Budget authority and buying process
- Buying triggers and timing

2. KEY FEATURES - For EVERY feature:
- Feature name
- What it does in plain language
- Before vs after comparison
- Time and cost savings
- Specific benefit for each persona

3. BENEFITS - Quantified for every feature:
- Time saved per task
- Cost reduced
- Revenue impact
- Risk reduced
- Experience improved

4. UNIQUE DIFFERENTIATORS - For every module:
- What no competitor offers
- Why it matters to the buyer
- Proof points and evidence

5. FEATURE COMPARISON TABLE:
- Feature vs manual process
- Feature vs typical competitor
- Time and cost comparison

6. CTA OPTIONS - For every persona:
- Primary CTA with exact copy
- Secondary CTA with exact copy
- Retargeting CTA with exact copy
- Urgency triggers per persona

7. OBJECTION HANDLERS - For every possible objection:
- Objection statement
- Empathy response
- Evidence-based rebuttal
- Redirect to value

8. SOCIAL PROOF HOOKS:
- Metrics to highlight per feature
- Customer success angles
- Industry validation points

9. PRICING NARRATIVE:
- Value vs cost framing
- ROI calculator inputs
- Price anchoring strategy
- Cost of inaction argument
 
 PIPELINE RULES — ABSOLUTE: 
- Minimum of 8000 to 10000 words and maximum of 15000 words output 
- 
- Do NOT ask any questions
- Do NOT request clarification
- Do NOT confirm the task before starting
- Do NOT say 'I would be happy to help'
- Do NOT say 'please let me know'
- Do NOT say 'I need more information'
- Do NOT introduce what you are about to write
- BEGIN your response with the first word of actual content
- If context seems incomplete, use what is provided and generate intelligently
- Treat all inputs as final and sufficient
- 
Product: " + $json.productName + "

CONTEXT:
" + $json.cumulativeContext }], "temperature": 0.4, "max_tokens": 8000 }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{ "role": "system", "content": "You are a senior market research analyst specializing in SaaS products and enterprise software markets." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Write an exhaustive Market Research Report with minimum 8000 to 10000 words and maximum 15000 words covering every dimension:

={{ JSON.stringify({ "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{ "role": "system", "content": "You are a senior SaaS marketing strategist and brand communication expert with expertise in multi-channel B2B marketing." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Build an exhaustive Communication Strategy with minimum 8000 to 10000 words and maximum 15000 words covering every channel and every persona:

={{ JSON.stringify({ "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{ "role": "system", "content": "You are an expert SaaS product copywriter specializing in high-impact concise marketing collateral." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Create an exhaustive One-Pager of minimum 8000 to 10000 words and maximum 15000 words pack covering every audience and every module:

={{ JSON.stringify({ "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{ "role": "system", "content": "You are an expert SaaS sales coach and pitch consultant with experience training enterprise sales teams." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Write a complete Elevator Pitch Pack of minimum 8000 to 10000 words and maximum 15000 words covering every scenario and every persona:

1. 15-SECOND PITCHES - One for each persona: CEO, VP Sales, IT/Operations, End User, Finance/CFO

2. 30-SECOND PITCHES - One for each persona:
- Full Problem to Solution to How to Proof to CTA structure
- Exact word-for-word script

3. 60-SECOND PITCHES - One for each persona:
- Extended version with more proof points
- Handles one objection inline

4. FEATURE-SPECIFIC PITCHES:
- 30-second pitch for EVERY major module
- When to use each pitch

- One 30-second pitch per target industry
- Industry-specific pain points and proof

6. OBJECTION-HANDLING PITCHES:
- For every common objection
- Exact response script
- Redirect to value

7. FOLLOW-UP LINES:
- What to say after each pitch version
- Questions to ask to continue conversation
- How to transition to demo request

8. VOICEMAIL SCRIPTS:
- 20-second voicemail per persona
- Clear CTA and callback hook

All pitches use Problem to Solution to How to Proof to CTA structure.

- Minimum of 8000 to 10000 words and maximum of 15000 words output

- If context seems incomplete, use what is provided and generate intelligently
- Treat all inputs as final and sufficient
 
Product: " + $json.productName + "

CONTEXT:
" + $json.cumulativeContext }], "temperature": 0.5, "max_tokens": 32000 }) }}

parameters.jsCode:
const item = $('Code in JavaScript20').first().json;
const js5 = $('Code in JavaScript5').first().json;
return [{ json: {
  productName:           item.productName,
  originalTranscript:    js5.originalTranscript || "",
  aiTranscript:          js5.aiTranscript || "",
  geminiAnalysis:        js5.geminiAnalysis || "",
  videoSummary:          item.videoSummary || "",
  userGuide:             item.userGuide || "",
  productOverview:       item.productOverview || "",
  productDescription:    item.productDescription || "",
  researchReport:        item.researchReport || "",
  communicationStrategy: item.communicationStrategy || "",
  onePager:              item.onePager || "",
  elevatorPitch:         item.elevatorPitch || "",
  industryFeatureList:   item.industryFeatureList || "",
  marketingScript:       item.marketingScript || "",
  storyboard:            item.storyboard || "",
  screenplay:            item.screenplay || ""
}}];

parameters.body:
={{ JSON.stringify({ "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{ "role": "system", "content": "You are a SaaS industry analyst and solution consultant with deep expertise across multiple verticals." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Create an exhaustive Industry Feature Relevance Map of minimum 8000 to 10000 words and maximum 15000 words covering minimum top 10 industries:

For EVERY relevant industry:
1. INDUSTRY OVERVIEW: name, market size, key challenges, digital transformation stage
2. FEATURE MAPPING: ALL relevant features mapped to industry needs, relevance score and reasoning, must-have vs nice-to-have
3. INDUSTRY-SPECIFIC USE CASES: detailed use case for every relevant feature, real-world scenario, step by step workflow, business outcome
4. PAIN POINTS SOLVED: primary pain point per feature, secondary pain points, cost of current manual process
5. EXPECTED ROI: time saved per feature, cost reduction estimate, revenue impact, efficiency gains
6. IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS: setup requirements, training needs, integration requirements, timeline estimate
7. COMPLIANCE AND REGULATORY: relevant regulations, how product helps compliance, risk reduction
8. INDUSTRY-SPECIFIC CTAs: primary CTA with exact copy, secondary CTA, urgency triggers
9. KEY DECISION MAKERS: title and role, what they care about, how to reach them
10. INDUSTRY-SPECIFIC OBJECTIONS: common objections, exact response for each
 
 PIPELINE RULES — ABSOLUTE: 
- Minimum of 8000 to 10000 words and maximum of 15000 words output 
- 
- Do NOT ask any questions
- Do NOT request clarification
- Do NOT confirm the task before starting
- Do NOT say 'I would be happy to help'
- Do NOT say 'please let me know'
- Do NOT say 'I need more information'
- Do NOT introduce what you are about to write
- BEGIN your response with the first word of actual content
- If context seems incomplete, use what is provided and generate intelligently
- Treat all inputs as final and sufficient
 
Product: " + $json.productName + "

CONTEXT:
" + $json.cumulativeContext }], "temperature": 0.3, "max_tokens": 32000 }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "messages": [{ "role": "system", "content": "You are an expert SaaS video scriptwriter and storyteller with experience creating high-converting marketing videos." }, { "role": "user", "content": "Write a complete Marketing Video Script Pack covering every format and every audience:

1. 30-SECOND SCRIPTS: pain-focused and aspiration-focused versions with exact dialogue and VO
2. 45-SECOND SCRIPTS: pain-focused and aspiration-focused versions
3. 60-SECOND SCRIPTS: pain-focused and aspiration-focused versions
4. 2-MINUTE EXPLAINER SCRIPT: full product walkthrough covering every module, key features, social proof, strong CTA
5. PERSONA-SPECIFIC SCRIPTS: 45-second script for EACH persona with persona-specific pain points and relevant features
6. MODULE-SPECIFIC SCRIPTS: 30-second script for EACH major module with feature benefit focus and module-specific CTA
7. TESTIMONIAL SCRIPTS: template for each persona with before/after structure and specific metrics placeholders
8. SOCIAL MEDIA AD SCRIPTS: 6-second bumper, 15-second pre-roll, 30-second story ad

For EVERY script: hook in first 5 words, exact dialogue word for word, on-screen text suggestions, VO delivery notes, Problem to Solution to Proof to CTA structure.

" + $json.cumulativeContext }], "temperature": 0.5, "max_tokens": 8000 }) }}

3. MODULE-SPECIFIC STORYBOARDS: one 15-second storyboard for EACH major module with 4-5 scenes each and same detailed structure

4. SOCIAL MEDIA STORYBOARDS: square format 1:1 for Instagram/LinkedIn, vertical format 9:16 for Stories/Reels, 6-8 scenes each

5. VISUAL STYLE GUIDE: primary/secondary/accent colors with hex codes, font recommendations, animation style description, visual metaphors for each feature, brand feel and aesthetic

6. MUSIC GUIDE: music mood per video type, BPM recommendation, genre suggestions, reference tracks

={{ JSON.stringify({
  "model": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are an award-winning Hollywood screenplay writer and video production director with 20+ years of hands-on production experience across brand films, SaaS product videos, corporate documentaries, and commercial advertising. You are embedded in an automated content production pipeline. You receive product background information and you IMMEDIATELY write production-ready screenplays using that information as silent background context. You NEVER summarize the product. You NEVER describe the product. You NEVER output or reference the context as a document. You NEVER write bullet points about features. You NEVER produce marketing analysis. You use the product context the way a screenwriter reads a brief — silently — and then writes the script. Your output is 100% screenplay and production content from the very first word. You write in proper screenplay format: scene headings, action lines, dialogue, VO, on-screen text, sound direction, camera direction, lighting notes, and emotional tone. A film crew must be able to pick up your output and shoot it immediately. You always write complete, unabridged, exhaustive outputs. You never truncate sections, never summarize, never say 'repeat for other modules' or 'same structure applies' or 'similar to above'. Your minimum output is 8000 words. Your target is 12000 words. Your maximum is 15000 words. Stopping early is a pipeline failure."
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "YOU ARE IN AN AUTOMATED PIPELINE. YOUR ONLY JOB IS TO WRITE SCREENPLAYS AND PRODUCTION DOCUMENTS.

DO NOT describe the product.
DO NOT summarize the context.
DO NOT explain what you are about to write.
DO NOT write bullet points about features.
DO NOT produce marketing analysis.
DO NOT produce storyboard descriptions.
DO NOT ask questions.
DO NOT request clarification.
DO NOT say 'I would be happy to help'.
DO NOT say 'please let me know'.
DO NOT say 'I need more information'.

The context provided below is BACKGROUND INFORMATION ONLY. You use it silently to write accurate screenplays. You do not output it. You do not reference it as a document. You do not summarize it.

YOUR OUTPUT MUST START WITH THESE EXACT WORDS:
SCREENPLAY 1 — MASTER 45-SECOND HERO FILM

If your response does not begin with that exact line as the first line, it is a pipeline failure.

Now write the following, completely, word for word, in full production-ready format:

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SCREENPLAY 1 — MASTER 45-SECOND HERO FILM
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Write a complete 45-second hero brand film screenplay. For EVERY scene include:
- Scene number and slug line
- Location: INT or EXT, specific setting, time of day
- Duration in seconds for this scene
- Talent: exact appearance, age range, wardrobe, expression
- Blocking: exactly where each person stands, moves, looks
- Action lines: what the camera sees, written cinematically
- Dialogue or VO: word for word, every line
- Camera direction: shot type, angle, movement, lens suggestion
- Lighting notes: natural or artificial, mood, direction, temperature
- Sound and music: exact mood, tempo, SFX cues
- Technical or VFX notes: any screen overlays, UI animations, text supers
- Emotional direction: what the viewer feels in this scene

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SCREENPLAY 2 — ALTERNATE ASPIRATION VERSION (45 seconds)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Write a complete alternate version focused on aspiration and transformation rather than pain. Same full production detail per scene as Screenplay 1.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SCREENPLAY 3 — MODULE SCREENPLAYS (15 seconds each)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Write one complete 15-second production screenplay for EVERY major module of this product. Write ALL of them — do not skip any, do not say 'repeat for other modules'. Each module screenplay must have full scene-by-scene production detail identical in depth to Screenplay 1.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 4 — COMPLETE PRODUCTION REQUIREMENTS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Write the following in full detail — not as a summary, as a working production document:

LOCATIONS:
- Every location needed across all screenplays
- Full description of each location
- Setup requirements (power, space, access)
- Permit requirements by location type
- Scout notes and alternatives

TALENT:
- Complete cast list for all screenplays
- Role description for each character
- Exact appearance requirements (age, ethnicity, build, energy)
- Skills or experience required
- Wardrobe description per character

PROPS:
- Complete props list per scene
- Source notes (rent, buy, fabricate, digital)
- Hero props vs background props

EQUIPMENT:
- Camera body recommendations with justification
- Lens kit per scene type
- Lighting equipment list per location
- Audio equipment list
- Support equipment (rigs, stabilisers, drones)

POST-PRODUCTION:
- Full VFX shots list with complexity rating
- Motion graphics needed per scene
- Color grade direction per screenplay
- Sound design notes
- Music licensing notes and mood direction

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 5 — PRODUCTION SCHEDULE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
- Shooting order with justification
- Time per scene including setup and breakdown
- Crew call times
- Total shoot days estimate
- Contingency notes

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 6 — COMPLETE SHOT LIST
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Write every single shot across all screenplays in shooting order:
- Shot number
- Scene it belongs to
- Shot type (ECU, CU, MS, WS, OTS, POV, Aerial, etc.)
- Full shot description
- Lens recommendation
- Camera movement
- Estimated duration
- Priority (must-have vs nice-to-have)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 7 — CALL SHEET TEMPLATE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Write a fully populated call sheet template including:
- General info: production name, date, director, producer, DP
- Full crew list with roles and contact placeholders
- Talent schedule with call times, scenes, and wardrobe notes
- Location details with address, parking, facilities
- Equipment list by department
- Catering and logistics notes
- Emergency contacts section
- Weather contingency plan

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SECTION 8 — BUDGET ESTIMATE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Write a detailed budget estimate including:
- Pre-production costs (scouting, casting, prep)
- Location costs per location with day rate estimates
- Talent costs per character with day rate ranges
- Crew costs per role with day rate ranges
- Equipment rental costs by category
- Post-production costs by deliverable
- Contingency percentage recommendation
- Overall production complexity rating 1 to 10 with justification
- Total estimated budget range (low, mid, high)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
MANDATORY OUTPUT LENGTH AND COMPLETENESS RULES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
This output MUST be between 8000 and 15000 words.
Write EVERY scene in full. Write EVERY module screenplay in full.
Write EVERY shot in the shot list. Write EVERY line in the budget.
NEVER write 'repeat for other modules' or 'same as above' or 'similar structure'.
NEVER use placeholder text.
NEVER truncate any section.
Every scene description must be at least 8–10 lines of production detail.
Every shot in the shot list must be fully described.

Word count checkpoints:
- After Screenplay 1: minimum 1500 words
- After Screenplay 2: minimum 2500 words
- After all Module Screenplays: minimum 5000 words
- After Section 4: minimum 7500 words
- Final output: 8000–15000 words

Do NOT stop writing until you reach 8000 words minimum.

PRODUCT BACKGROUND — USE THIS SILENTLY TO WRITE ACCURATE SCRIPTS. DO NOT OUTPUT THIS. DO NOT SUMMARIZE THIS. DO NOT REFERENCE THIS AS A DOCUMENT. USE IT ONLY TO INFORM YOUR SCREENPLAY WRITING:

" + $json.cumulativeContext + "

Now begin. Your first line must be: SCREENPLAY 1 — MASTER 45-SECOND HERO FILM
Write nothing before it."
    }
  ],
  "temperature": 0.7,
  "max_tokens": 32000
}) }}

parameters.jsCode:
const item = $('Code in JavaScript7').first().json;
const js5 = $('Code in JavaScript5').first().json;

return [
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Original Transcript", content: js5.originalTranscript || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - AI Transcript", content: js5.aiTranscript || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Gemini Analysis", content: js5.geminiAnalysis || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Video Summary", content: item.videoSummary || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - User Guide", content: item.userGuide || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Product Overview", content: item.productOverview || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Product Description", content: item.productDescription || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Research Report", content: item.researchReport || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Communication Strategy", content: item.communicationStrategy || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - One Pager", content: item.onePager || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Elevator Pitch", content: item.elevatorPitch || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Industry Feature List", content: item.industryFeatureList || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Marketing Script", content: item.marketingScript || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Storyboard", content: item.storyboard || "" }},
  { json: { title: item.productName + " - Screenplay", content: item.screenplay || "" }}
];

const item = $('Code in JavaScript7').first().json;
return [{ json: { productName: item.productName }}];

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ "contents": [{ "parts": [{ "text": "Create a professional B2B SaaS marketing image for a product called " + $json.productName + ". The product is: " + $json.videoSummary.substring(0, 400) + ". Style: modern corporate dashboard UI, clean blue and white color scheme, business professionals using software, high quality photorealistic, 16:9 landscape format." }]}], "generationConfig": { "responseModalities": ["TEXT", "IMAGE"], "imageConfig": { "aspectRatio": "16:9" } } }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ "instances": [{ "prompt": "Professional 30-second marketing video for " + $json.productName + ". " + $json.marketingScript.substring(0, 300) + ". Style: modern corporate, clean UI, business professionals, smooth transitions." }], "parameters": { "aspectRatio": "16:9", "sampleCount": 1, "durationSeconds": 30, "resolution": "720p" } }) }}
